card.intuition: Scan left-to-right maintaining a decreasing stack of 'waiting' indices — the moment a larger element arrives, it answers all waiting elements in the stack that are smaller.
card.analogy: People waiting in line for the first person taller than them. As a tall person walks past, everyone shorter who was waiting behind them finally gets their answer simultaneously.
card.space_complexity: O(n) for the stack and result array
when_to_use: Daily temperatures — how many days until a warmer day?
Stock span — how many consecutive days with price <= today?
Next greater element in a circular array (iterate 2n elements modulo n)
Any problem asking: for each element, find the first larger element to the right
gotchas: Elements remaining in the stack after the main loop have no next greater element — they stay -1 (or 0 for stock span)
For circular arrays, iterate indices 0..2n-1 modulo n and only record results for the first n iterations
The stack stores indices (not values) so you can compute positional differences (e.g., number of days)

exercise.prompt: Given daily temperatures, return an array where result[i] = number of days until a warmer temperature. Use 0 if no warmer day exists.
hints: When temps[i] is warmer than the day at the top of the stack (index j), the wait is i - j days
Pop index j, compute i - j, store it in result[j], then continue popping while the condition holds
exercise.test_code:
assert daily_temperatures([73,74,75,71,69,72,76,73]) == [1,1,4,2,1,1,0,0]
assert daily_temperatures([30,40,50,60])              == [1,1,1,0]
assert daily_temperatures([30,60,90])                 == [1,1,0]
assert daily_temperatures([90,80,70,60])              == [0,0,0,0]
assert daily_temperatures([70])                       == [0]
print('PASS')
